name: Insider Threat Investigation
description: Investigates potential insider threats with a multi-stage workflow that balances security urgency with legal and HR compliance. An initial AI analysis assesses risk level. High-risk activity requires HR and Legal approval before proceeding. Upon approval, a code node gathers comprehensive user activity data (login history, file access, email patterns), followed by a focused AI analysis for data exfiltration indicators. If exfiltration is detected, the user account is disabled, forensic evidence is preserved via a Jira task, and HR/Legal are notified. If no exfiltration is found, a monitoring ticket is created. Low-risk alerts are annotated and closed.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: Microsoft Graph
required_integrations.reason: Gather user activity data and disable user accounts upon confirmed exfiltration

required_integrations.name: Jira
required_integrations.reason: Create forensic preservation tasks and monitoring tickets for ongoing investigations

config.description: Triggers when an insider threat, data exfiltration, or policy violation alert is ingested

config.additional_instructions: Assess the risk level of the reported insider activity. Determine if this warrants a full investigation or is standard user behavior.
config.description: AI-powered insider threat assessment to determine risk level and investigation priority
Route based on whether the AI assessment determined high-risk insider activity requiring full investigation
Both HR and Legal must approve the investigation scope before user activity data is collected
Collect 30 days of user login history, file access patterns, and email activity from Microsoft Graph

config.additional_instructions: Analyze user activity for data exfiltration indicators, policy violations, and anomalous behavior patterns.
config.description: AI-powered data exfiltration analysis examining user activity patterns for indicators of data theft or policy violations
Route based on whether the AI analysis found evidence of data exfiltration or confirmed policy violations
Immediately disable the user account in Azure AD to prevent further data exfiltration

Required Actions:

2. Capture endpoint forensic image
3. Export file access audit logs
4. Preserve cloud storage activity
5. Document chain of custody

config.description: Create a Jira task to formally track forensic evidence preservation for legal proceedings
